template_name: Standard Prescription Format – Heel Prick in Neonates
indications: Newborn metabolic screening (CH, CAH, PKU, G6PD etc.)

preparation: Wash hands and wear sterile gloves
Warm heel (37–40°C for 3–5 minutes) to improve blood flow
Position baby comfortably (preferably held by caregiver)
site_selection: Use medial or lateral plantar surface of heel
Avoid center and posterior heel (risk of bone injury)
sample_collection: Wipe away first drop (may be contaminated)
Collect subsequent drops on filter paper or capillary tube
Apply gentle intermittent pressure (avoid excessive squeezing)
post_procedure: Apply gentle pressure with sterile gauze until bleeding stops
Apply small sterile dressing if required

prevention_of_complications: Correct site selection (avoid center of heel)
post_procedure_advice: Mild discomfort is normal and temporary
No special care required at puncture site
Observe for redness, swelling, or discharge
red_flags: Signs of poor perfusion or lethargy
parent_counseling: Heel prick is safe and essential for early detection of serious diseases
Early screening helps prevent long-term disability
Report any swelling, redness, or persistent bleeding
counselling: Heel prick is safe and essential for early detection of serious diseases — एड़ी की चुभन सुरक्षित है और गंभीर बीमारियों की जल्दी पहचान के लिए ज़रूरी है
Pain is minimal and short-lasting — दर्द बहुत कम और थोड़ी देर का होता है
Proper technique prevents complications — सही तरीके से करने पर कोई समस्या नहीं होती
Early screening helps prevent long-term disability — जल्दी जांच से लंबी अक्षमता रोकी जा सकती है
Report any swelling, redness, or persistent bleeding — अगर सूजन, लालिमा या लगातार खून बहे तो बताएं
Breastfeeding during procedure reduces pain — जांच के दौरान दूध पिलाने से दर्द कम होता है
warning_signs: Persistent bleeding from puncture site — चुभन की जगह से लगातार खून बहना
Swelling or redness suggesting infection — संक्रमण की ओर इशारा करती सूजन या लालिमा
Excessive crying or irritability — बहुत ज़्यादा रोना या चिड़चिड़ापन
Signs of poor perfusion or lethargy — कमज़ोर रक्त प्रवाह या सुस्ती के संकेत
expected_course: The heel prick site heals within 1-2 days. Mild redness at the puncture site is normal and resolves within hours. Results of newborn screening tests are typically available within 1-2 weeks. Abnormal or borderline results require repeat testing.
key_clinical_points: Optimal timing: 48-72 hours after birth (after adequate feeding)
Use medial or lateral plantar surface of heel only — avoid center (bone injury risk)
Warm the heel for 3-5 minutes before puncture to improve blood flow
Puncture depth must not exceed 2 mm
Wipe away first drop — collect subsequent drops
Avoid excessive squeezing — causes hemolysis and false results
Non-pharmacological pain relief: skin-to-skin, sucrose, breastfeeding during procedure
Screens for: CH, CAH, PKU, G6PD, sickle cell disease, galactosemia

monitoring_parameters.frequency: Check at 1 hour and next feed for bleeding or hematoma
Review within 1-2 weeks of sample collection
If inadequate sample, <24 hours sample, or borderline results
